heroHeadline: Day 45: CJNG Co-founder El 85 Pleads Guilty in U.S.; O3 Succession Holds as Semana Santa Security Winds Down
heroSubtitle: Day 45 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco. CJNG co-founder Érick Valencia Salazar ('El 85') pleaded guilty on April 7 in U.S. federal court in Washington D.C. to cocaine trafficking — closing a major chapter in the prosecution of CJNG's founding generation. O3 (Juan Carlos Valencia González) continues his succession consolidation under a collective council structure, backed by Grupo Élite's paramilitary capacity. El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) remains at large as the primary rival power center with a $5 million U.S. State Department bounty. Operativo Semana Santa Segura 2026 runs through April 12. Operativo Enjambre cumulative total: 67+ officials detained.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, NCTC, U.S. Treasury (OFAC), InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Wilson Center, Reuters, AP, Wall Street Journal, El País, Washington Post, France 24, CGTN, Al Jazeera, Latin Times, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, WOLA, Milenio, Excélsior, Proceso, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: April 8, 2026.
